sections: What Contract Delivery Means for Your Business

A contract delivery service is a standing arrangement in which we handle your outbound deliveries on a regular, pre-agreed basis rather than as one-off bookings. For furniture retailers, kitchen and bathroom showrooms, antique dealers and interior designers across Sussex, this turns the unpredictable headache of ‘how do we get this to the customer?’ into a settled, dependable part of your operation. As a family-run Sussex removals & storage company founded in 2016, we have spent years refining the logistics of moving bulky, valuable and fragile goods, and that same discipline underpins every contract round we run.

Under a contract, you get an agreed schedule, agreed pricing and a delivery team who already understand your products, your packaging and your standards. There is no re-explaining the job every week. Whether you need a fixed weekly route, a multi-drop run on set days, or capacity reserved for your busy periods, we build the arrangement around how your business actually works. If you are unsure which structure suits you, our team is happy to talk it through — just request a tailored delivery quote and we will map out the options.

Heavy and awkward items are where most generic couriers fall down. A two-seater sofa, a solid-oak wardrobe, an American-style fridge-freezer or a marble-topped dining table all demand more than a single driver and a trolley. Our crews are trained to lift, carry and place these items safely, protecting both the goods and your customer’s home. We come equipped with blankets, straps, floor protection and the experience to navigate tight staircases, narrow Sussex doorways and period properties without a scratch.

Because we are also a full removals company, our standards for handling are far higher than a parcel network’s. The same care we apply to a white-glove service move flows into every contract delivery. For genuinely high-value or delicate stock, we can combine delivery with our bespoke custom crate service so pieces travel fully protected and arrive in showroom condition.

  • Sofas, beds, wardrobes, tables and flat-pack assembly drop-offs
  • White goods including fridges, freezers, washing machines and range cookers
  • Mirrors, glass, artwork and antiques handled with blankets & corner protection
  • Floor and doorway protection laid before every carry-in

Many of our contract rounds run with a two-person team, and for good reason. A second pair of hands is not a luxury when you are delivering heavy furniture into a customer’s first-floor bedroom — it is the difference between a clean, safe install and a damaged item or an injured back. Two-person delivery also means we can carry pieces in rather than leaving them on the doorstep, which protects your brand and reduces the after-sales complaints that come from kerbside drops.

For lighter or single-item runs, a streamlined man-and-van delivery option may be the more economical choice, and we are happy to mix and match across a contract so you are never paying for more crew than a given route requires. The goal is always the right team for the job, every time. Retailers who already trust us with larger logistics often layer this in alongside our commercial removals service for a single, accountable supplier.

Reliability is the whole point of a contract. Your customers have been promised a delivery, and a missed or vague slot reflects on your business, not ours — which is exactly why we treat your reputation as our own. We operate to agreed timed windows wherever your routes allow, and we keep your customers informed so they are not left waiting in all day. Our modern, fully maintained fleet keeps breakdowns and no-shows to a minimum, and every job is fully covered under our £10m liability insurance for genuine peace of mind.

We are LAPADA members and Checkatrade-verified, and we are recommended by respected names including Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart. That track record is built on showing up when we say we will. You can read more about our background and values on our about-us page, or call the team directly on 01903 893731 to discuss the service levels your contract needs.

  • Agreed timed delivery windows on routed days
  • Customer notifications so recipients know when to expect us
  • Fully covered work under £10m liability insurance
  • Consistent crews who learn your products and standards
How a Contract Delivery Arrangement Works

Setting up is straightforward. We start with a short conversation about your typical volumes, item types, delivery areas and the days that matter to you. From there we propose a schedule and a clear, transparent rate — you can see how we approach costs on our pricing page. Once agreed, we lock in your route and assign the crew, vehicles and any specialist equipment your stock requires.

Ongoing, you simply hand us the deliveries and we handle the rest: loading, routing, customer contact, carry-in and proof of delivery. If your volumes grow or seasonal demand spikes, we scale the arrangement up; if a quiet spell arrives, we flex it down. For stock that needs to wait between manufacture and dispatch, we can fold in business and commercial storage so we collect, hold and deliver as a single, joined-up service.
Who Our Contract Delivery Service Suits

This service is built for businesses that send out bulky or valuable goods on a recurring basis and want one trusted partner instead of a rotating cast of couriers. Furniture and homeware retailers, kitchen and bedroom showrooms, antique and fine-art dealers, interior designers, appliance suppliers and manufacturers all benefit from a delivery team that treats their stock with the same care they do.

We are based at Doryln House, London Road, Ashington, near Pulborough RH20 3JT, and we deliver across West Sussex, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, with reach throughout the UK and into Europe when your customers are further afield. If you are a Sussex business weighing up your delivery logistics, get in touch on 01903 893731 or email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk and we will design a contract that fits.
faqs: How regular do my deliveries need to be to qualify for a contract?

There is no rigid minimum. Some clients run a fixed weekly route, others book a set day each fortnight or reserve capacity for seasonal peaks. The arrangement is built around your real volumes, so whether you send out a handful of large items each week or several multi-drop runs, we can structure a contract that makes sense. Speak to us on 01903 893731 and we will recommend a schedule.
Will the same crew handle my deliveries each time?

Wherever possible, yes. Consistency is one of the main advantages of a contract — a regular crew learns your products, your packaging and the standards your customers expect, which means smoother deliveries and fewer issues. For very high-value or fragile stock we can pair delivery with our custom crate service for added protection in transit.
Can you carry items into the room of choice, not just the doorstep?

Absolutely. Our two-person teams are trained to carry furniture and white goods into the customer’s chosen room, lay floor protection and place items safely — the same care we apply on our white-glove service. Room-of-choice delivery protects your brand and cuts down on after-sales complaints from kerbside drops.
Do you cover deliveries outside Sussex?

Yes. While we are rooted in West Sussex and serve East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, our fully maintained fleet handles deliveries across the UK and into Europe. If your customer base extends beyond the South East, request a quote and we will scope a contract that covers your full delivery area.
